dcFatalities.fatalitySource: DC MPD / Vision Zero crash data

rideHailVMTShare.note: Ride-hail share of city-core VMT. Conservative estimate of 10% assumes AV ride-hail captures most of existing ride-hail market plus modest growth.

waymo.source: Kusano et al., Traffic Injury Prevention (2025), 56.7M miles

rideHailSafetyComparison.caveat: Methodologies differ across these sources. Waymo data uses police-reported incidents matched to location baselines; Uber/Lyft figures are self-reported. Direct comparison should be interpreted with caution.

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2024)
studies.finding: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 7.1M autonomous miles

studies.name: Kusano et al. (2025)
studies.finding: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes, 79% fewer any-injury-reported crashes over 56.7M miles

studies.name: Swiss Re / Waymo (2024)
studies.finding: Waymo vehicles had zero bodily injury claims vs. human baseline

permitTimeline.note: Testing since Apr 2024 — still no commercial permit

timeline.event: DC passes Autonomous Vehicle Testing Program law
timeline.detail: Mandates DDOT complete a safety study by late 2022
Safety study not completed; regulatory limbo begins
AV deployment blocked without completed study

timeline.event: Waymo begins testing in DC with safety drivers
timeline.detail: Limited testing only — no public ride-hail service
Bill to enable AV deployment in DC
No AV ride-hail service permitted in DC

sources.name: NHTSA FARS
sources.description: Fatality Analysis Reporting System — national traffic fatality data

sources.name: DC Vision Zero
sources.description: District of Columbia traffic safety initiative and crash data

sources.name: Open Data DC
sources.description: DC government open data portal — crash and fatality datasets

sources.name: Kusano et al. (2024)
sources.description: Peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer any-injury-reported crashes in 7.1M Waymo miles

sources.name: Swiss Re / Waymo Insurance Study (2024)
sources.description: Zero bodily injury claims for Waymo fleet vs. human baseline

sources.name: B26-0323 — Autonomous Vehicle Deployment Act
sources.description: Proposed DC legislation to permit AV ride-hail deployment

sources.name: Fehr & Peers Ride-Hail VMT Study (2019)
sources.description: Ride-hail accounts for 1–13% of city-core VMT across major US cities (DC: 6.9%, SF: 12.8%)

sources.name: Kusano et al. (2025) — 56.7M Miles
sources.description: Updated peer-reviewed study: 85% fewer suspected serious injury+ crashes over 56.7M Waymo miles
